macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: IMF Releases World Economic Outlook: Global Growth Downgraded to 3.1% Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: The IMF downgraded global growth to 3.1% for this year from January forecasts, with headline inflation rising to 4.4%, citing Middle East war impacts including Strait of Hormuz closure, sharp rises in oil, gas, and commodity prices. Adverse scenarios project growth as low as 2% and inflation over 6% if disruptions persist.[1][3]

items.title: Fed Governor Barr Highlights Surging Commodity Prices Impacting US Agriculture
items.summary: Fed Governor Barr noted diesel prices up 50% over the past year and fertilizer costs rising 55% due to Middle East conflict disrupting Strait of Hormuz, alongside lower US soybean exports to China and higher tariffs pressuring farmers.[4]

items.title: Upcoming US Economic Data and Fed Chair Powell Speech on April 14
items.summary: Key US releases included Chicago PMI at 52.8, S&P/Case-Shiller Home Price MoM at -0.1%, with Fed Chair Powell scheduled to speak; other data like ISM Manufacturing PMI, JOLTs Job Openings, and PCE Price Index also featured in the economic calendar.[2]

items.title: IMF Upgrades Brazil Growth Forecast to 1.9% Despite Global Risks
items.summary: IMF raised Brazil's growth forecast to 1.9% this year, supported by strong momentum, improved terms of trade, and high renewable energy share mitigating energy shock impacts from Middle East disruptions.[3]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 5% to $75,000 Amid US-Iran Peace Signals
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 5% over the past 24 hours to around $75,000, nearing its highest level since early February, driven by market optimism over potential US-Iran peace talks following President Trump's signals for renewed discussions with Tehran.

items.title: Ethereum Leads Rally with 7% Gain to $2,400 Two-Month High
items.summary: Ethereum outperformed major tokens, jumping 7% to nearly $2,400 and marking a two-month high, as crypto markets rallied on easing geopolitical tensions and strong institutional inflows.

items.title: $525 Million in Leveraged Short Positions Liquidated
items.summary: Over $525 million in leveraged short positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, marking one of the largest events in history, amid Bitcoin topping $75,000 and total market cap climbing to $2.63 trillion.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Rises 4.5% to $2.52 Trillion on Risk Appetite Recovery
items.summary: The total crypto market capitalization increased 4.53% to $2.52 trillion, fueled by recovering risk appetite in traditional markets like Nasdaq and $1.1 billion in ETP inflows, with top performers including Algorand (+9.4%) and Aptos (+6.2%).

items.title: BitMine Accumulates 71,524 ETH, Reserves Exceed 4.8 Million
items.summary: BitMine acquired 71,524 ETH worth $156.4 million last week, pushing its reserves over 4.8 million ETH or 4.04% of Ethereum's total supply, highlighting strong institutional accumulation.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Mythos with Advanced Security Capabilities, Withholds Public Release
items.summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Mythos, a new model capable of finding and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities in major operating systems and browsers. The company is withholding public release and instead launching Project Glasswing, a consortium giving early access to Microsoft, Apple, Google, and AWS to patch systems before the capability becomes widely available.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning Transforms AI Agent Capabilities with GRPO and RLVR Techniques
items.summary: Industry pioneers from Prime Intellect, Unsloth, and NVIDIA discussed how reinforcement learning is evolving AI agents beyond supervised fine-tuning, highlighting emerging techniques like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for environment-grounded alignment.

items.title: AI Agent Landscape Exposed as More Fragile Than Marketing Suggests
items.summary: Industry analysis from the AI Agents Summit revealed that while AI agents are increasingly capable, the field faces significant fragility issues. Experts noted that 2025 exposed gaps in professional engineering practices rather than intelligence limitations.

items.title: Claude Code Emerges as Powerful Design Tool for Rapid Application Development
items.summary: Claude Code combined with advanced embedding models is enabling developers to build interactive designs and applications in minutes using systematic approaches, marking a new era in AI-assisted software development.

items.title: Dark Factory Demonstrates Fully Autonomous AI Code Generation and Review
items.summary: A live demonstration showed an AI coding agent writing, reviewing, and merging its own code without human intervention, powered by the Archon orchestration platform, building a RAG-powered agent platform autonomously.
items.source: Nate B Jones AI News & Strategy Daily

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Warhammer Classics Label Announced, Reviving Delisted Games on Steam
items.summary: A new Warhammer Classics label is bringing back delisted titles like Shadow of the Horned Rat, Final Liberation, Space Hulk, and others to Steam after resolving licensing issues. This initiative celebrates the return of classic Warhammer 40k games previously removed from digital storefronts.

items.title: Guild Wars 2 Launches Super Adventure Festival 2026 with New Patch and Content
items.summary: Guild Wars 2 released patch notes on April 14, 2026, including the return of Super Adventure Box with a new level, test zone, holographic weapons, and rewards until May 5. The update features balance changes and warnings for third-party add-ons.

items.title: X4: Foundations 9.00 'The Empire Update' Enters Beta, Hailed as Top Space Sim of 2026
items.summary: The latest beta update for X4: Foundations enhances space simulation gameplay, making it a standout title early in 2026 according to player reviews.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Ising, World's First Open AI Models for Quantum Computing
items.summary: NVIDIA announced Ising, a family of open-source quantum AI models that improve quantum processor calibration and error correction, up to 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate than industry standards. Adopted by leading labs including Harvard, Fermilab, and UK's NPL to advance scalable quantum systems.

items.title: New Adaptive Deep Brain Stimulation Technology Offers Hope for Parkinson's Patients
items.summary: A new adaptive deep brain stimulation technology is transforming lives for Parkinson's patients, as reported by NBC News, with a patient diagnosed in her 30s describing life-changing benefits.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Breakthrough Analog AI Chip for Efficient Computing
items.summary: IBM Research introduced a groundbreaking analog AI chip that achieves high efficiency and accuracy in complex deep neural network computations.
